title: Proposal for Glyph Studios Private Limited
opening_paragraph: At Verita Corporate Counsel, we understand that for an animation and motion design studio such as Glyph Studios — three co-founders, fourteen months of operating history, and a credible seed conversation already in motion — the absence of a founders agreement is the single largest avoidable diligence risk on the table. Pursuant to our recent discussions, this proposal sets out a sequenced two-phase mandate to put a clean, fair, investor-ready founders agreement on paper and follow it with a 12% ESOP that captures the studio's creative talent.
approach_bullets: Convert the verbal 40-40-20 split into a written three-founder agreement with back-credited vesting
Address the seed-round 'red flag' by building IP assignment, exit clauses, and drag-along/tag-along into the agreement
Sequence the ESOP to start once the founders agreement is in revisions, so total turnaround is approximately three weeks
Deliver a 12% ESOP plan covering the first cohort of 6–8 creative hires, with full statutory and operational backing
scope_section_title: Scope of Services — Founders Agreement & ESOP Implementation
scope_section_intro: We propose two phases, sequenced so the ESOP plan references the post-founders-agreement cap table.

phases.name: Phase I: Founders Agreement
phases.framing_sentence: Verita Corporate Counsel will draft a three-founder agreement that converts your verbal 40-40-20 split into investor-ready paper, with vesting back-credited for the 14 months you've already been operating.
scope_bullets: Drafting of a three-founder agreement modeling vesting (4-year schedule, 1-year cliff, back-credited 14 months) for all three founders
IP assignment from each founder to the company (existing and going-forward)
Decision-making framework: simple majority for ordinary matters; unanimity for reserved matters (equity issuance, dilution, sale of assets)
Good-leaver and bad-leaver exit provisions with buyback at fair value or formula price
Drag-along and tag-along provisions to align with seed-round investor expectations
Up to two iterations of revisions based on founder inputs
outcome_bullets: Investor-grade founders agreement addressing the seed-round red flag
Transparent vesting outcomes modeled for each founder under multiple departure scenarios

phases.name: Phase II: ESOP Plan & Implementation
phases.framing_sentence: Once Phase I is in revisions, Verita Corporate Counsel will draft a 12% ESOP plan and grant infrastructure tailored to a creative studio where talent is the asset.
scope_bullets: Drafting of ESOP plan covering eligibility, vesting, exercise price determination, plan administration, and treatment on termination
Letter of Grant template covering options granted, exercise price, vesting schedule, expiration, and other key terms
Drafting of board and shareholder resolutions for plan approval and grant of options
MGT-14 filing for the special resolution
Tracking spreadsheet for grant, vesting, and exercise
outcome_bullets: ESOP framework aligned with seed-round investor diligence expectations
Operational tooling for ongoing grant administration and vesting tracking

deliverables: Founders agreement — investor-ready, signed by all three founders
Vesting model showing each founder's outcome under good-leaver / bad-leaver scenarios
ESOP plan, Letter of Grant template, board and shareholder resolutions, MGT-14 filing
ESOP grant, vesting, and exercise tracking spreadsheet
Diligence-ready folder of executed and filed documents

cost_table.tat: 10 working days (in parallel from Phase I revisions)

exclusions_note: Government filing fees (MGT-14 ~INR 600), stamp duty on the founders agreement, and applicable taxes are excluded and charged at actuals. Payment terms: 50% advance to commence work; balance on delivery of each phase.
next_steps: Project Confirmation — confirmation of acceptance taken over email
Engagement Agreement — sent across by Verita Corporate Counsel
Work in Progress — Phase I drafting commences on receipt of 50% advance; Phase II commences in parallel once Phase I is in revisions
Delivery — both phases delivered within approximately three weeks
